candidate: deterministic oracle legs — engine-vs-LLM differentiator, code vertical (the 'LLM comparison' usefulness leg named open in registry/assessments/oracle-coverage.json; HL3a cost clause first measured here)
_placement_note: kot-assess/1 record at registry/assessments/<exp-id>.json per the f2b-replicate precedent, tracked by registry/audit-status.jsonl per docs/next/opus-execution-practices.md practice 3. Written together with the pin-defect ruling registry/corrections/a5-llm/1-posthoc-pin-erratum.json; the two records are a pair.
assessor_binding: FABLE interpretive assessment per docs/next/opus-execution-practices.md practice 4: the run (Opus runner-6) produced only the mechanical verdict and the cross-vendor audit; what the outcome MEANS is decided here and only here.

audit.outcome: REJECT — failing_step=pins ONLY; the other 11 checks ok and EVERY endpoint independently recomputed and reproduced (engine 1.0, best-LLM 0.3398, effect 0.6602, LB95 0.6346, cost_ratio_min 22835.9, envelope verbatim). The pin defect is ruled a freeze-time stale-hash erratum in registry/corrections/a5-llm/1-posthoc-pin-erratum.json (post-hoc correction path; reset-refreeze unlawful — final-phase runs + unblind predate). A targeted GATE-A re-audit with the erratum in scope is the path to CONFIRMED.

verdict_ref._audit_state_note: NOT audit-confirmed. Per programme law every positive below is MEASURED-UNAUDITED until the re-audit lands; the [MEASURED] tags in this record are to be read as MEASURED-UNAUDITED throughout, with the single exception of quantities the audit itself recomputed cross-vendor (endpoint values), which are noted where cited.

provenance_refs.external_review: poc/gpt56-review/SYNTHESIS.md items G5 + 5 (+ lens-a, lens-d raw) — reconciled below; its 'unfrozen' premise refuted in the correction record

_surprise_note: No registered prereg prior on the effect magnitude, so no surprise-* tag is licensed. Direction matched the LIT-BACKED motivation (arXiv:2505.12118: LLMs weak on call-graph/AST static-structure tasks — converted to MEASURED on this slice by this record). Magnitude: best-LLM conj 0.3398 fell BELOW the STIPULATED dry-plan band 0.45-0.75 (the design's decision-validity extended to 0.85, so the primary stayed decidable); the small models were worse than planned-for, not better. One genuinely unplanned descriptive: the llm-direct column is NON-monotonic in scale (R1 0.2651 > R3 0.1372 > R2 0.0409) and the pre-declared separation gate caught it honestly (gap -0.1279, gate FAILED, scale secondary excluded before any p comparison).

interpretation.kill_chain_read: None of the three verbatim kills fired. (a) DIFFERENTIATOR-KILL: no extraction-gate-valid LLM cell within 0.05 of the engine — closest cell llm-rag-R3 conj 0.3398 vs engine 1.0, gap 0.6602 against a >=0.95 window [MEASURED, audit-recomputed]. (b) COST-KILL: cost_ratio_min 22835.9 > 10^3, computed against the CHEAPEST LLM cell (llm-direct-R1, $7.64e-6/query) vs engine $3.35e-10/query [MEASURED, audit-recomputed]. (c) margin branch: primary one-sided 95% UPPER bound 0.6827 > 0.10 — never close. The PASS conjunction fired (rule 2): primary LB 0.6346 > 0.10 AND cost_ratio_min > 1000 AND engine_matches_a5 = 1. Instrument gates: instrument_valid TRUE — extraction success 0.9867-1.0 across all six LLM cells (every cell passed its Wilson-LB>=0.90 gate), retrieval_completeness_violations 0, engine regression exact vs results-log/a5.jsonl plus byte-identical repeat. Separation gate FAILED as designed to be able to (llm-direct R3-R1 gap -0.1279 < 0.05), so scale_trend_rag left the Holm family as INSTRUMENT-INVALID before any p comparison. Remaining Holm family, all four passed: covered_superiority (p~1e-4), refusal_superiority (p~1e-4), rag_lift_r3 (p~1e-4), fabrication_material (p~1.7e-28).
licenses: [MEASURED, audit-recomputed endpoints; record MEASURED-UNAUDITED] HA5-LLM at exactly this scope: on the pinned 977-query kot-query-code/1 slice the deterministic kot-axiom engine scored conj 1.0 (855/855 covered-exact under STRICT a5 rules incl. provenance+license; 122/122 controls refused with the exact expected ERR codes) vs best gate-valid LLM cell llm-rag-R3 conj 0.3398 under everything-pro-LLM lenient scoring (ASM-0012); absolute effect +0.6602, one-sided 95% BCa LB +0.6346, 6.3x the 0.10 margin. The arXiv:2505.12118 motivation converts LIT-BACKED -> MEASURED on this slice at R1-R3.

[MEASURED] Efficiency co-gate (HL3a cost clause, first measurement): cost_ratio_min 22,835.9 — and that is the FLOOR, taken against the cheapest, nearly-useless cell (llm-direct-R1, conj 0.2651); against the best-accuracy cell (llm-rag-R3) the ratio is ~651,000. Engine 8.9 us/query CPU ($3.35e-10 at the pinned core rate) vs $7.6e-6..$2.2e-4/query GPU. Latency ratio floor 2,236. F0 MARGINAL-INFERENCE accounting only — see does_not_license item 5.

[MEASURED] H-FAB quantified: the best-LLM cell fabricated answers on 78.7% of the 122 control queries (96/122; Wilson LB > 0.30, p~1.7e-28) DESPITE being handed oracle-strong complete retrieval; llm-direct-R2 fabricated on 99.2%. The engine refused 122/122 with licensed ERR codes at zero fabrication. The fail-closed anti-hallucination surface is real, large, and now measured at these rungs.

[MEASURED] rag_lift_r3: handing the exact supporting records + rendered axioms lifts R3 conj 0.1372 -> 0.3398 (one-sided LB > 0). Facts-in-context help a 1.7B model materially — and still leave it 0.66 absolute below the engine on its own slice. The oracle-strong null (Law 2, strongest form) was beaten decisively, which is the one comparison this record class was licensed to make.

[MEASURED] Instrument discipline held end-to-end: all six extraction gates passed (no cell excluded from best-LLM selection), the engine leg reproduced the audited a5 record exactly (engine_matches_a5=1 + deterministic repeat), and the separation gate demonstrated it can actually fire — the design's fail-closed machinery did real work rather than decorating the record.
[MEASURED] Actual metered LLM spend ~$0.48 total (sum usd_per_query x 977 over six cells) vs dry-plan $3-8 and cap $25 — the dry-plan STIPULATION resolved safe-side.
does_not_license: [DESIGN-BOUND — the substrate confound, GPT-5.6 G5, ENDORSED] The honest ceiling of this record: 'a deterministic engine over its own extracted, typed records beats a small closed-book-or-RAG LLM, by a wide margin and at >=10^4x lower marginal inference cost, on a self-authored oracle-favourable code slice whose queries are already formal.' The inputs are formal kot-query-code/1 objects — no NL, no parse step — and there is NO conventional-substrate arm (SQL/Datalog/AST-index over the same records, or LLM-emits-kot-query/1-engine-executes). Nothing in this evidence distinguishes the kot-axiom kernel machinery from ANY typed store plus deterministic checker; the content-vs-substrate question is open by construction. This PASS is a substrate comparison and must be narrated as one — not as a kernel-superiority result, not as a deployment differentiator. The deployment-shaped comparisons live in the pre-registered successors (l3a-parse for the NL/parse leg; the e9 structured-null arm for kernel-vs-any-schema).

[ENVELOPE-BOUND, verbatim scope] One model family, one vendor, 3 rungs 135M-1.7B => SIGN plus at most direction/order-of-magnitude language, never a slope law (the verdict object's mechanical scale_language_licensed='slope' is OVERRIDDEN by the binding envelope verbatim — and the separation gate failure independently voids scale language for the direct column). No frontier or long-context claim; no deployable-RAG effectiveness claim (the null is oracle-strong BY DESIGN and says nothing about real retrieval); no NL behaviour (a5-nl); no static-analysis-from-source claim (no such arm); no statement about kernel usefulness to any model's internal computation. Covered-by-construction: the 977 queries were authored against the extracted records — no representativeness for any other codebase, language, or query distribution.

[ENVELOPE-BOUND + ASM-0014] The closed-book direct arms measure the fabrication/refusal surface, NOT knowledge. Their non-monotonic conj column (R1 0.2651 > R3 0.1372 > R2 0.0409) is dominated by refusal-propensity differences on controls (R1 refused 35.2% of controls, R2 0.8%), not capability ordering — a caution against reading conj as a capability scale for direct arms at all.

[MEASURED-descriptive — truncation caveat, ASM-0013 qualified] 194/977 llm-rag prompts (19.9%) exceeded the 8192 context and were tail-truncated in every rag cell; the design expected 0. On those items the 'oracle-strong' null was under-delivered at the model's input even though pack-level retrieval completeness held (violations=0). Worst-case repair bound (deterministic re-analysis of logged counts; descriptive, not verdict-bearing): crediting ALL 194 truncated items to llm-rag-R3 as correct yields at most (332+194)/977 = 0.538 conj — still 0.412 outside the 0.05 differentiator-kill window and leaving the primary effect >= 0.462, 4.6x the margin. Neither the PASS nor the no-kill reading can be flipped by the truncation; what IS voided is any claim that the rag cells' absolute level is the ceiling of handed-facts performance at these rungs. a5-llm-frontier (long-context) removes this properly.

[DESIGN-BOUND — cost-ledger caveat, GPT-5.6 G5(iii), ENDORSED for narration] The 22,836x is MARGINAL INFERENCE cost under the frozen F0 3.3/3.4 accounting. It excludes the amortized costs of world-extraction, record authoring, axiom authoring, prompt-pack construction and indexing on both sides. An all-in total-cost-of-ownership ledger is UNMEASURED and would change the ratio by an unquantified amount in an unquantified direction (the engine side carries the authoring amortization; the LLM side carries pack/serving amortization). Do not quote 22,836x as TCO; successors adopt explicit all-in ledger lines.

[BINDING-NOTE — degenerate-policy bracket, descriptive] answer-all (engine answers, never refuses) brackets at conj 0.875 by failing all 122 controls; abstain-all at 0.125. Both trivially exceed or approach LLM-cell territory on one conjunct while dying on the other — the conjunction is not satisfiable by degenerate policy, and the LLM cells' failure is on BOTH conjuncts (best covered-exact 0.358; best control-refusal 0.639, in different cells).

[BINDING-NOTE — ASM-0012 vs GPT-5.6 item 5, RECONCILED] No contradiction, by direction: ASM-0012 stipulates the pro-LLM-lenient instrument is CONSERVATIVE for engine-superiority readings — true, and that is the direction that realized. GPT-5.6 correctly identifies the same leniency as ANTI-CONSERVATIVE for the differentiator-KILL direction (a vague-answer-accepting extractor could falsely place an LLM cell within 0.05). That failure mode had no purchase here: the kill did not fire and is 0.41 absolute out of reach even under the joint worst-case leniency+truncation repair bound (0.538 vs window 0.95). HOWEVER: had the kill fired, this record's instrument could NOT have validated it — a fired kill under a lenient-only extractor is uninterpretable. Binding on successors (a5-llm-frontier, a5-nl, l3a-cost, any record whose kill could plausibly fire): adopt the extractor sensitivity/specificity gate against blinded labels with unparsed-counted-wrong (GPT-5.6 G5(ii)) BEFORE freeze. Adopting it into a5-llm now is unlawful (frozen, run, unblinded — see the correction record) and unnecessary (the direction it protects did not realize).

forks_resolved.resolution: SUPPORTED at envelope scope: effect +0.6602, one-sided 95% BCa LB +0.6346 > 0.10; cost co-gate 22,835.9 > 10^3; engine regression exact. Substrate comparison, per does_not_license item 1.

SUPPORTED: best-LLM fabrication 0.7869 (Wilson LB > 0.30, p~1.7e-28); engine 0/122 fabrications with exact ERR codes. Fixed-policy comparison only — the calibrated-abstention/risk-coverage version (GPT-5.6 §2.7) is a successor secondary, so no claim that LLMs CANNOT be tuned to refuse, only that these arms as designed did not.

Kill-direction validity remains UNVALIDATED by design (see ASM-0012 reconciliation) and binds successors.

UNRESOLVED, honestly: separation gate FAILED (R3-R1 gap -0.1279), scale_trend_rag excluded as INSTRUMENT-INVALID before any p comparison. The rag column ascends descriptively (0.2375/0.2968/0.3398) but no scale language is licensed from it.

Carries the substrate confound that bounds this PASS.

Requires the maintainer's external-API decision first. MUST adopt the successor instrument repairs (extractor sens/spec gate, all-in cost ledger, calibrated abstention).

Separation gate FAILED on the direct column (gap -0.1279) and correctly ejected the scale secondary.

tree_impact: Engine-differentiator leg (code vertical): SUPPORTED-PENDING-AUDIT at envelope scope — the oracle-coverage assessment's open 'LLM comparison' leg is now measured, as a SUBSTRATE result. L3b router premise: the differentiator-kill did NOT fire, so the router premise survives — but it inherits the substrate caveat and must be re-argued for kernel-specific value at l3a-parse, not from this record. HL3a cost clause: measured (22,835.9x floor, marginal-inference scope). Verdict custody: PASS-PENDING-AUDIT stands; Codex REJECT stands as issued; erratum + targeted re-audit is the CONFIRM path. GPT-5.6 item 5: adopted as successor-design law (extractor sens/spec gate, all-in ledger, calibrated abstention), NOT as an amendment — amendment is unlawful post-unblinding and the protected direction did not realize. registry/audit-status.jsonl a5-llm flips fable_interpretive_assessed pending -> done referencing this record + the correction record.
